name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: Farmer welfare scheme by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare to provide income support to all landholding farmers' families in the country to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
eligibility_text: EligibilityAll landholding farmers' families, which have cultivable land holding in their names are eligible to get benefit under the scheme.
documents: Aadhaar Card | Landholding papers | Savings Bank Account.

name: Kisan Credit Card
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: The Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme was introduced in 1998 for issue of Kisan Credit Cards to farmers on the basis of their holdings for uniform adoption by the banks so that farmers may use them to readily purchase agriculture inputs and draw cash for their production needs.
eligibility_text: EligibilityEligibilityFarmers - individual/joint borrowers who are owner cultivators;Tenant farmers, oral lessees & share croppers;Self Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability Groups (JLGs) of farmers including tenant farmers, share croppers etc
documents: Application Form. | Two Passport Size Photographs. | ID proof such as Driving License / Aadhar Card / Voter Identity Card / Passport. | Address Proof such as Driving License, Aadhar Card. | Proof of landholding duly certified by the revenue authorities. | Cropping pattern (Crops grown) with acreage.

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojna (PMFBY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture. PMFBY aims to provide financial protection to farmers against crop loss due
benefit: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana was launched in 2016 with the aim to support farmers by providing an affordable comprehensive risk cover for crops.
eligibility_text: EligibilityAll farmers, including tenant farmers and sharecroppers growing notified crops in notified areas.Farmers must have an insurable interest in the insured crops.Farmers must possess a valid and authenticated land ownership certificate or a valid land tenure agreement.The farmer must be a cultivator or sharecropper on the insured land.The farmer must apply for insurance coverage within the prescribed time frame, usually within 2 weeks of the start of the sowing season.Farmers must not hav
documents: Passport–sized Photograph | Passbook | Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Voter ID/Bank Passbook with Photo, Kisan Photo Book/NREGA Job Card) | Address Proof (Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Voter ID/Electricity Bill/Bank Passbook with Photo). | Evidence of Land records, as per the State Records of Right (RoR), Land Possession Certificate (LPC), and other applicable agreements, contracts notified or permitted by the state. | Declaration about the crop sown/intended to be sown.

name: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
ministry: Ministry of Education PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment Child
benefit: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Education.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe applicant must be a citizen of India.The applicant must be studying in a Primary or Upper Primary class (Class 1st to Class 8th) in a Government or a Government-aided school.
documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Mark sheet / Bonafide certificate | Passport photo

eligibility_text: EligibilityFor PMEGP new enterprises (Units) Any individual, above 18 years of age. There will be no income ceiling for assistance in setting up projects under PMEGP. name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme-2
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) launched the National Apprenticeship Promo
benefit: To promote apprenticeship training in the country, by providing partial stipend support to the apprentices engaged under the Apprentice Act, 1961, undertaking capacity building of the apprenticeship ecosystem, and providing advocacy assistance to the stakeholders.
eligibility_text: EligibilityMinimum age for an apprentice is 14 years, and 18 years for designated trades related to hazardous industries, as per Section 3(a) of the Apprentices Act, 1961. Apprenticeship training is primarily a transition from academia to workplace, and therefore, to avail partial stipend support by the Government, at the time of registration the upper age limit should be 35 years. Candidates from skill development programmes such as Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDUGKY), Nation

eligibility_text: EligibilityFinance is provided for Greenfield Enterprises.If the applicant is a male, he must be from SC / ST category.The age of the applicant must be at least 18 years.The applicant must not be in default to any bank/financial institution.

name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India, aims to provide cashless hospitalisation
benefit: AB PM-JAY is a health insurance scheme for low-income families in rural and urban areas. The scheme aims to provide affordable healthcare facilities to the Poor. It is the largest health assurance scheme in the world which aims at providing a health cover of ₹5,00,000/- per family per year.

documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Passport photo

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India, aims to provide free and cashless delive
benefit: The scheme aims to provide completely free and cashless delivery, including caesarean section, for pregnant women at government health facilities along with free treatment for sick newborns and infants.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe applicant must be a pregnant woman.The applicant must be admitted to a government health facility.﻿

name: Soil Health Card Scheme - Uttarakhand
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: The scheme aims to provide Soil Health Cards to all farmers in the state, helping them understand the nutrient composition of their soil and the type and quantity of fertilizers suitable for their crops.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.The applicant must be a farmer.The farmer must own agricultural land in the state and be actively engaged in farming activities.﻿
documents: Aadhar card | Residence certificate | Mobile number

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) to provide handholding support to street vendors. The
benefit: PM SVANidhi is a micro-credit scheme launched on 1st June 2020 by the MoHUA to provide handholding support to street vendors. The scheme facilitates a working capital collateral-free loan of ₹10,000, with subsequent loans of ₹20,000 and ₹50,000 with a 7% interest subsidy.

name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. It is a non-contributory pension scheme to provi
benefit: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL) of the society.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe eligibility criteria under Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is:The applicant must be a widow in the age group of 40-79 years.The applicant should belong to a household living below the poverty line according to the criteria prescribed by the Govt. of India﻿
documents: Death Certificate of husband – With the name of the surviving wife(widow). | BPL Card. | Age Proof - For age, the birth certificate or school certificate may be relied on. In their absence ration card and EPIC may be considered. If there is no valid document, any Medical Officer of any government hospital may be authorized to issue the age certificate.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: A National Mission for Financial Inclusion (NMFI), namely, Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) was launched by the Prime Minister across the country on 28th August, 2014. It envisages to provide universal banking services for every unbanked adult in the country.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe applicant should be an Indian National.If minors above ten years apply, they will require support from their legal guardians for administering their PMJDY account.
documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Passport photo

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) is an insurance scheme offering life insurance cover for death due to any reason. It is a one-year cover, renewable from year to year. The scheme is offered by banks/post offices and administered through life insurance companies.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe age of the applicant must be between 18 to 50 Years.The applicant must hold an individual bank / post office account.
documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Bank passbook | Passport photo

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: An Accident Insurance Scheme offering accidental death and disability cover for death or disability on account of an accident.
eligibility_text: EligibilityIndividual bank account holders of participating banks aged between 18 years (completed) and 70 years (age nearer birthday) who give their consent to join / enable auto-debit, will be enrolled into the scheme.
documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Bank passbook | Passport photo

name: Atal Pension Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) is an old age income security scheme for a savings account holder in the age group of 18-40 years, who is not an income tax-payee. It helps to address longevity risks among workers in unorganized sector and encourages the workers to voluntarily save for retirement.
eligibility_text: EligibilityAge of joining and contribution periodThe minimum age of joining APY is 18 years and maximum is 40 years.The age of exit and start of pension is 60 years.Subscriber contribution to APY shall be made through the facility of ‘auto-debit’ of the prescribed contribution amount from the savings bank account of the subscriber on monthly, quarterly or half-yearly basis.The subscribers are required to contribute the prescribed contribution amount from the age of joining APY till the age of 60
documents: Aadhaar card | Caste certificate | Bank passbook | Passport photo

name: Senior Citizens Saving Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Electronics & IT () Government of India
benefit: Introduced in 2004 by the Govt. of India, SCSS offers a steady stream of income for individuals over 60 years old. As it is a government-backed scheme, there is minimal risk associated with SCSS. Individuals can apply for this scheme in post offices and public and private banks.
eligibility_text: EligibilityIndian citizens above the age of 60 yearsRetirees in the age bracket of 55-60 years who have opted for Voluntary Retirement Scheme (VRS) or Superannuation*Retired defense personnel above 50 years and below 60 years of age*﻿(*Investment must be made within a month of availing the retirement benefits.)
documents: KYC Documents: Aadhaar Card, Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Passport | Utility Bills: Telephone bill, Electricity bill | Senior Citizen Card or Birth Certificate (Case Specific) | 2 passport-size photographs

name: Raah-Veer Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH). The MoRTH Evaluation Committee selects the top 10 R
benefit: The scheme encourages citizens to help road accident victims during emergencies by providing a ₹25,000 incentive to individuals who save lives through timely medical assistance.
eligibility_text: EligibilityThe applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.The applicant should be any individual (citizen or resident) who voluntarily assists a road accident victim in a motor vehicle-related accident.The applicant must have helped the victim reach a hospital within the Golden Hour, ensuring the victim’s survival.﻿
documents: Acknowledgment Slip from Police Station (Issued on official letterhead, containing details of the Rah-Veer, date, time, location, and assistance provided) | Verification Report from the Hospital/Doctor (Confirming that the victim was brought for treatment within the Golden Hour) | Identity Proof | Bank Account Details | Mobile Number | Address Proof

benefit: The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship among Scheduled Caste individuals by providing concessional finance. Benefits include investment ranging from ₹10,00,000/- to ₹15,00,00,000/- as debt or equity for eligible Private Limited or Public Limited companies.

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana

benefit: The scheme is to provide maternity benefits to women belonging to socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society.
